## Sweep Context

- Date:
- Vantage:
- Product surface:
- Hub issue:

## Sources Reviewed

- [ ] Open refinement issues
- [ ] Active child issues
- [ ] Recent pull requests
- [ ] Recent daily sweeps
- [ ] Product docs or architecture notes
- [ ] Relevant user, sales, support, or operational evidence

## Candidate Findings

| Priority | Finding | Size | Evidence | Duplicate check |
| -------- | ------- | ---- | -------- | --------------- |
| P1       |         |      |          |                 |

## Promotion Gate

| Finding | Bucket | Target issue | Reason |
| ------- | ------ | ------------ | ------ |
|         |        |              |        |

Allowed buckets: `Ignore`, `Watch`, `Merge`, `Promote to parent`,
`Promote to child`, `Escalate`.

## Capacity Check

- [ ] Weekly parent issue budget checked.
- [ ] Weekly child issue budget checked.
- [ ] Escalations are justified if they bypass capacity.

## Decisions

-

## Created Or Updated Issues

-

## Closeout

- [ ] Rejected and deferred findings are captured here.
- [ ] Promoted findings link to parent or child issues.
- [ ] Duplicate findings update the surviving issue.
- [ ] Hub issue is updated with this sweep link and summary.
- [ ] No implementation work starts directly from this sweep.

## Codex Thread Protocol

- Reuse the daily sweep Codex thread recorded in `codex-thread-map`.
- The sweep thread may create or update GitHub issues after the promotion gate.
- Implementation child threads must be spawned from the relevant parent issue
  thread, not directly from this sweep.
